fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 12-year-old male brought by mother for anxiety about germs and contamination.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 12-year-old male presenting with increasing anxiety about germs and contamination over past six months. He reports excessive handwashing up to 20 times daily, particularly after touching doorknobs, and refuses to use public restrooms. He denies any thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation. Patient states he fears getting sick and spreading germs to his family. Hands are red and chapped from frequent washing. He spends significant time ensuring items are clean before use. Behavior intensified after classmate had serious illness earlier this year. Academic performance maintained but social activities limited due to contamination fears. Refuses to eat food prepared by others outside family. Mother reports patient was always cautious but current behaviors are excessive and distressing to patient. He recognizes behaviors are excessive but cannot stop. (Psychiatrist Dr. Benjamin White, MD)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: No suicidal or homicidal ideation; contamination obsessions

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents and younger sister; contamination-focused OCD with excessive handwashing and avoidance; triggered by peer's illness; good insight; social and functional impairment emerging.
Assessment_and_Plan: OCD with contamination obsessions and washing/avoidance compulsions; functional impairment and medical consequences (skin damage).
Protective factors: good insight, supportive family, maintains academic performance, no suicidal ideation.
Referral for specialized OCD treatment with exposure and response prevention (ERP).
Consider SSRI trial given moderate severity and functional impairment.
Family education on not accommodating compulsions while being supportive.
Outpatient psychiatry follow-up in 2 weeks for medication consideration.
Monitor for symptom escalation or spreading to other domains.
fields.Author: Dr. Benjamin White, MD (Psychiatrist)
